workbench_thesis.thesis: The product is shaping into a compliance evidence and exception-preparation engine, not an autonomous compliance operator.
workbench_thesis.reason: The three-slice pattern summary says the next shared build sequence is a reviewer workbench for assist-lane steps where confirmation, edits, and rationale visibility matter most.

supporting_steps.step_name: Classify whether the item is an advertisement
supporting_steps.build_implication: Best handled as an assistive classifier that shows why the item likely is or is not an advertisement, while routing social fragments, event remarks, and linked-content edge cases to human review.

supporting_steps.step_name: Flag testimonials, ratings, and performance content
supporting_steps.build_implication: Strong assist candidate: automate red-flag detection and disclosure reminders, but treat OCR or richer media parsing as an enhancement layer rather than a blocker to human-confirmed review.

supporting_steps.step_name: Draft the review memo and remediation path
supporting_steps.review_action: Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.
supporting_steps.build_implication: Assist candidate: draft issue lists, redline suggestions, and remediation checklists, but keep humans in charge of the actual judgment call and let memo style live in firm-specific templates.

supporting_steps.step_name: Reconcile registration basis and profile fields
supporting_steps.build_implication: Build assistive comparison tooling that flags changed eligibility and profile fields, but keep the final registration-basis conclusion with a reviewer.

supporting_steps.review_action: Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.
supporting_steps.build_implication: Build redline and change-detection support for brochure sections, but keep final narrative judgment and disclosure calls in a reviewer-confirmed assist lane.

supporting_steps.step_name: Prepare the readiness memo and exception list
supporting_steps.review_action: Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.
supporting_steps.build_implication: Build memo-drafting and issue-rollup support, but keep reviewer confirmation prominent because this step still packages judgment for the signer.

supporting_steps.workflow_name: RIA code-of-ethics personal trading exception review
supporting_steps.step_name: Confirm access-person scope and reporting obligations
supporting_steps.build_implication: Build a reviewer-visible access-person classifier that flags edge cases and records the basis for inclusion or exclusion instead of making hidden scope decisions.

supporting_steps.workflow_name: RIA code-of-ethics personal trading exception review
supporting_steps.step_name: Review trades, preclearance, and restricted-list exceptions
supporting_steps.review_action: Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.
supporting_steps.build_implication: Build a reviewer workspace that assembles restricted-list, preclearance, and client-trade context for each flag instead of auto-concluding that every hit is a violation.

supporting_steps.workflow_name: RIA code-of-ethics personal trading exception review
supporting_steps.step_name: Draft the exception memo and remediation package
supporting_steps.review_action: Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.
supporting_steps.build_implication: Build reviewer-editable memo drafts and remediation suggestions, but keep conclusions, tone, and any disclosure call fully in human hands.

pilot_steps.step_name: Draft the review memo and remediation path
pilot_steps.review_action: Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.
pilot_steps.build_implication: Assist candidate: draft issue lists, redline suggestions, and remediation checklists, but keep humans in charge of the actual judgment call and let memo style live in firm-specific templates.

pilot_steps.step_name: Prepare the readiness memo and exception list
pilot_steps.review_action: Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.
pilot_steps.build_implication: Build memo-drafting and issue-rollup support, but keep reviewer confirmation prominent because this step still packages judgment for the signer.

pilot_steps.workflow_name: RIA code-of-ethics personal trading exception review
pilot_steps.step_name: Review trades, preclearance, and restricted-list exceptions
pilot_steps.review_action: Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.
pilot_steps.build_implication: Build a reviewer workspace that assembles restricted-list, preclearance, and client-trade context for each flag instead of auto-concluding that every hit is a violation.

shared_jobs_to_be_done.job: Show why the selected step is in the assist lane, not the automate or keep-human lane.
Let the reviewer inspect the evidence notes, supporting artifacts, and score rationales without leaving the step context.
Support reviewer edits to the memo, classification basis, or exception framing before anything moves forward.
Capture a visible reviewer outcome such as confirm, revise, or escalate, along with the reason for that choice.

v1_sections.title: Assist queue
v1_sections.purpose: Prioritize assist-lane steps across the locked workflows by build priority and reviewer caution.

v1_sections.title: Selected step context
v1_sections.purpose: Explain the workflow, owner, step description, and why the product currently recommends assist.

v1_sections.title: Evidence and artifacts
v1_sections.purpose: Keep the source-backed evidence, linked artifacts, and score rationales visible while the reviewer works.

v1_sections.title: Draft and rationale workspace
v1_sections.purpose: Give the reviewer one place to edit the classification basis, disclosure call, or memo framing before they decide how to proceed.

v1_sections.title: Reviewer outcome
v1_sections.purpose: Capture confirm, revise, or escalate outcomes with an explicit reason and prepare a package for the next human gate when needed.

explicit_non_goals: Do not collapse the final supervisory step into the workbench; that remains approval support and audit traceability only.
Do not turn this into a workflow router or a multi-workflow dashboard before the assist-lane interaction model is proven.
Do not add autonomous reviewer outcomes in v1; the workbench should prepare and record human judgment, not replace it.
acceptance_signals: A reviewer can open any pilot assist step and immediately see why it needs review.
A reviewer can inspect all source-backed evidence and linked artifacts without leaving the selected step.
A reviewer can edit the working rationale or draft output before recording an outcome.
A reviewer can explicitly confirm, revise, or escalate the step with a written reason.
The resulting package can be handed to the final human gate without adding hidden logic.
